report_name: Defense Contractor LOGZONE Pays $507K to Settle Claims of Falsifying Cybersecurity Compliance

identities.name: LOGZONE, Inc.
identities.description: An Alabama-based defense contractor that agreed to pay $507,144 to settle allegations of violating the False Claims Act by misrepresenting its compliance with Pentagon cybersecurity requirements (NIST SP 800-171) on U.S. Navy contracts.

identities.name: U.S. Navy
identities.description: The government entity that awarded contracts to LOGZONE, which required compliance with DFARS and NIST SP 800-171 for the protection of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI).

identities.name: Department of Justice
identities.description: The U.S. government agency that alleged LOGZONE violated the False Claims Act and announced the settlement as part of its Civil Cyber-Fraud Initiative.

identities.name: Defense Industrial Base Cybersecurity Assessment Center
identities.description: The government assessment center (DIBCAC) that conducted a 2024 assessment of LOGZONE's cybersecurity compliance, resulting in a score of -170.

identities.name: Defense Industrial Base
identities.description: The collective of thousands of contractors who handle Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) for the Department of Defense, for whom the LOGZONE settlement serves as a warning and precedent.
